title: allium narcissiflorum subsp. insubricum
intro: Allium narcissiflorum subsp. insubricum is a lesser-known, edible wild garlic species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It belongs to the Allium genus, which includes various types of garlic and onions. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Allium narcissiflorum subsp. insubricum can be used to make teas, infusions, and oils, offering potential health benefits such as boosted immune systems and reduced inflammation.
